summary:
The face is typically divided into six segments in transfacial proportion for assessing aesthetics and guiding reconstructive procedures. These include the forehead, cheeks, nose, upper jaw, lower jaw, and chin—standard regions used in both medical and cosmetic evaluations. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{6}}$

They identify those zones clearly – forehead, cheeks, nose, upper jaw, lower jaw, and chin—and conclude there are six such areas because these form part of accepted standards in facial evaluation and treatment planning.
